subject: [PATCH v3 05/20] btrfs: convert iomap ops to ->iomap_next()

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch converts the Btrfs direct I/O operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is generated by a macro that wraps the iomap_iter_next helper and finishes previous mappings as needed.

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Approved the patch with Acked-by, indicating no objections or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, Jul 20, 2026 at 02:01:47PM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> Convert btrfs iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback. The callback is
> generated with the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macro, which wraps the
> iomap_iter_next() helper to finish the previous mapping if needed and
> produce the next one. No functional changes are intended.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>

Acked-by: David Sterba <dsterba@suse.com>

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch converts the Btrfs iomap operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is generated by a macro that wraps the iomap_iter_next() helper.
